title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
This analysis examines how newspapers across 11 countries and regions frame and report on the Iran–Israel conflict during the period January 4 – April 4, 2026.
We collected articles from 55 major outlets using the GDELT Global Knowledge Graph, extracted full text via web scraping, and ran multilingual sentiment analysis using XLM-RoBERTa — a transformer model trained on 100+ languages. This allows us to analyze Italian, Spanish, French, German, and Portuguese articles in their native language without losing linguistic nuance through translation.

aljazeera.com


Middle East
Israel
English
Jerusalem Post, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Ynet, Israel National News







Data collection: GDELT 2.0 GKG queried via Google BigQuery, filtered by source domain and Iran/Israel/War themes.
Article scraping: Full text extracted with trafilatura; paywalled articles use available snippets.
Sentiment model: cardiffnlp/xlm-roberta-base-sentiment-multilingual — 3-class classification (positive / neutral / negative). Long articles chunked into 510-token windows with 50-token overlap; probabilities aggregated by token-weighted average.
Validation: BERT sentiment cross-validated against GDELT’s built-in tone scores.

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Dataset Summary


Code
df |>
  group_by(country, outlet_name) |>
  summarise(
    articles    = n(),
    avg_words   = round(mean(word_count, na.rm = TRUE)),
    date_min    = min(published_date, na.rm = TRUE),
    date_max    = max(published_date, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(country, desc(articles)) |>
  kbl(
    col.names = c("Country", "Outlet", "Articles", "Avg Words", "First", "Last"),
    caption   = "Article counts and coverage per outlet"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Articles per Country


Code
df |>
  count(country, scrape_status) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(country, n), y = n, fill = scrape_status)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Scrape status") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Articles per country",
    x = NULL, y = "Article count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Weekly Coverage


Code
df |>
  count(week) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4472C4") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "2 weeks", date_labels = "%b %d") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Articles per week (all countries)",
    x = NULL, y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Language Validation


Code
df |>
  group_by(country, language, detected_language) |>
  summarise(articles = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(match = ifelse(substr(language, 1, 2) == substr(detected_language, 1, 2), "✓", "✗")) |>
  arrange(country) |>
  kbl(
    col.names = c("Country", "Expected lang", "Detected lang", "Articles", "Match"),
    caption   = "Language validation summary"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"))

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Sentiment Heatmap
Country × sentiment dimension (avg BERT probabilities).


Code
country_avg |>
  pivot_longer(c(positive, neutral, negative), names_to = "sentiment", values_to = "prob") |>
  mutate(sentiment = factor(sentiment, levels = c("positive", "neutral", "negative"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sentiment, y = reorder(country, net), fill = prob)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(prob, 2)), size = 3.5, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "plasma", name = "Avg probability") +
  labs(
    title = "Average BERT sentiment probabilities by country",
    x = "Sentiment class", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"))

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Net Sentiment by Outlet


Code
corpus_mean <- mean(df$bert_net, na.rm = TRUE)

outlet_avg |>
  mutate(
    direction = ifelse(net > corpus_mean, "above average", "below average"),
    outlet_name = reorder(outlet_name, net)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = net, y = outlet_name, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = corpus_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~country, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("above average" = "#2196F3", "below average" = "#F44336"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Net sentiment (positive − negative) by outlet",
    subtitle = "Dashed line = corpus mean",
    x = "Net sentiment score", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Sentiment Distribution (Ridge Plot)


Code
df |>
  mutate(country = reorder(country, bert_net, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bert_net, y = country, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2, rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "cividis", name = "Net sentiment") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of net sentiment (positive − negative) by country",
    x = "Net sentiment score", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0622

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
section: Diverging Bar: Most vs Least Positive Outlets
text:
Diverging Bar: Most vs Least Positive Outlets


Code
outlet_avg |>
  mutate(
    label = paste0(outlet_name, " (", country, ")"),
    label = reorder(label, net)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = net, y = label, fill = net > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "#1976D2", "FALSE" = "#D32F2F"),
    labels = c("Net positive", "Net negative"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Outlets ranked by net sentiment",
    x = "Net sentiment (positive − negative)", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Model Confidence by Country
GDELT tone scores are not available via the Doc API (they are only computed in the BigQuery GKG pipeline), so cross-validation against GDELT is not possible here. As an alternative quality check, the chart below shows the distribution of BERT prediction confidence — the probability assigned to the winning class — for each country. High median confidence (> 0.8) indicates the model is making clear, unambiguous predictions rather than borderline calls.


Code
df |>
  mutate(
    confidence = pmax(bert_positive, bert_neutral, bert_negative, na.rm = TRUE),
    country    = reorder(country, confidence, FUN = median, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = confidence, y = country, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 1.8, rel_min_height = 0.01, quantile_lines = TRUE, quantiles = 2) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "magma", name = "Confidence", direction = -1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0.3, 1)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "BERT prediction confidence by country",
    subtitle = "Vertical line = 0.80 threshold · median marked by quantile line",
    x = "Confidence (probability of predicted class)", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0434

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
section: TF-IDF: Most Distinctive Words per Country

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Weekly Sentiment Trends by Country


Code
weekly <- df |>
  group_by(country, week) |>
  summarise(
    net = mean(bert_net, na.rm = TRUE),
    n   = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p <- weekly |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = net, color = country, group = country)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo", name = "Country") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "2 weeks", date_labels = "%b %d") +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly average net sentiment by country",
    subtitle = "Net sentiment = BERT positive − negative probability",
    x = NULL, y = "Net sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# Add event annotations if any exist
if (nrow(events) > 0) {
  p <- p +
    geom_vline(
      data = events,
      aes(xintercept = date),
      linetype = "dotted", color = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.7
    ) +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = events$date,
      y = max(weekly$net, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.9,
      label = events$label,
      angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 3, color = "firebrick"
    )
}

p

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
section: Small Multiples: Sentiment per Outlet Over Time
text:
Small Multiples: Sentiment per Outlet Over Time


Code
weekly_outlet <- df |>
  group_by(country, outlet_name, week) |>
  summarise(net = mean(bert_net, na.rm = TRUE), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

weekly_outlet |>
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = net, fill = net > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(country ~ outlet_name, scales = "free_x", ncol = 5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#1976D2", "FALSE" = "#D32F2F"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b") +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly net sentiment per outlet",
    x = NULL, y = "Net sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 6)
  )

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Sentiment Volatility by Country
Which countries show the most inconsistent sentiment week-to-week?


Code
weekly |>
  group_by(country) |>
  summarise(
    mean_net = mean(net, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_net   = sd(net, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_net, y = sd_net, label = country)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = sd_net), color = "#1976D2", alpha = 0.8) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(size = 4) +
  scale_size_continuous(guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment volatility by country",
    subtitle = "X = average stance, Y = week-to-week consistency (higher = more volatile)",
    x = "Mean net sentiment", y = "SD of weekly net sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Conclusions
[To be written after running the analysis — summarize key findings about sentiment differences across geographies, notable outliers, and temporal patterns.]

title: Media Sentiment on the Iran–Israel Conflict
text:
Reproducibility
All code is available at github.com/andbbus/war-sentiment.
To reproduce:
# 1. Install Python dependencies
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

# 2. Collect GDELT data (requires GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS)
python -m src.collect.gdelt_query

# 3. Scrape full article text
python -m src.collect.scraper

# 4. Preprocess
python -m src.preprocess.pipeline

# 5. Run sentiment inference
python -m src.sentiment.inference

# 6. Validate and export summary
python -m src.sentiment.export

# 7. Render Quarto report
quarto render analysis/report.qmd
